ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for coleslaw as a prepared food item; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large serving of coleslaw at Wingstop Singapore is approximately 200 g, served as a chilled side dish in a cup alongside fried chicken or wings.

nutrient_source.title: Coleslaw, with dressing — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172944)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for coleslaw with dressing is used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent a standard creamy mayonnaise-dressed coleslaw made from shredded cabbage and carrot, which matches the Wingstop product description. Per-100g values are taken directly from this entry.

gi_source.title: Coleslaw GI estimated from cabbage-based salad with mayonnaise dressing — analogous to low-GI non-starchy vegetable salads; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for creamy coleslaw was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 38 is estimated based on the low-GI profile of non-starchy vegetables (cabbage, carrot) combined with a mayonnaise dressing that contributes fat and minimal additional glycemic carbohydrates. Cabbage has a GI of approximately 10 and carrots approximately 35–47 (raw); the mayonnaise dressing lowers the overall glycemic response. An aggregate GI of 38 (low category) is defensible and consistent with published values for dressed vegetable salads in Atkinson et al. 2008. Glycemic load per serving = (38 × 11.4 × 200/100) / 100 = 8.7.

hypertension.guidance: A 200 g large serving of coleslaw provides approximately 426 mg sodium, representing about 18% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; portion control and requesting low-sodium dressing variants is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Coleslaw has a low estimated GI of 38 and a glycemic load of approximately 8.7 per large serving, making it a suitable side dish for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard portions without additional sugary dressings.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat and saturated fat from mayonnaise dressing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 200 g serving provides approximately 23 g total fat and 3.4 g saturated fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the mayonnaise-based dressing and consider reduced-fat alternatives to limit saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coleslaw is a cold salad made primarily from finely shredded raw green cabbage and grated carrot, dressed with a creamy mayonnaise-based sauce that typically includes vinegar, sugar, and salt. At Wingstop Singapore, coleslaw is served as a chilled side dish in a large cup, complementing the restaurant's fried chicken wings. The texture is crisp and crunchy from the raw vegetables, with a creamy, slightly tangy and sweet flavour profile from the dressing. Nutritionally, a large 200 g serving provides approximately 304 kcal, 2.4 g protein, 23 g fat, and 22.8 g carbohydrate. The dish is a source of dietary fibre (3 g per serving), vitamin C (44 mg), and vitamin A (94 mcg RAE) from the cabbage and carrot. The sodium content of approximately 426 mg per serving warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. The low glycemic index (estimated 38) makes coleslaw a relatively diabetes-friendly side option. It is vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free, though it contains egg and mustard from the mayonnaise. Coleslaw is widely consumed across Western fast-food contexts and is a standard accompaniment to fried chicken in American-style restaurant chains operating in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Wingstop coleslaw?
Can I eat coleslaw if I have high blood pressure?
How much fat is in coleslaw?
What is the GI of coleslaw?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from the USDA SR Legacy exact entry for coleslaw with dressing (FDC ID 172944), which is a strong match for a standard creamy coleslaw. The GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed published tables rather than a direct measurement for this specific product. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Serving size of 200 g is estimated for a large cup serving at Wingstop Singapore; no official gram weight was published on the field survey listing. USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172944 used as primary nutrient source — exact match for coleslaw with dressing. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) based on the low-GI profile of non-starchy vegetables with a fat-rich dressing; no direct GI measurement for creamy coleslaw was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — coleslaw contains no animal-derived ingredients other than egg in mayonnaise, and egg yolk contributes negligible B12 at this serving level; USDA reports 0.00 mcg per 100g). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — USDA SR Legacy reports 0.0 mcg vitamin D for coleslaw with dressing). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy). Ontology: FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_00002473 is used as the best available match for coleslaw; FoodEx2 code A00LC is used as a defensible match for coleslaw/cabbage salad with dressing. SNOMED CT has no specific concept for coleslaw as a prepared food and is set to null with gap note. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. Halal status confirmed from Wingstop Singapore's halal certification as listed on field survey. Allergens include egg and mustard from mayonnaise. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172944 and are not sentinel values.
